response_language.description: The natural language selected from the newest customer message, or the language explicitly requested by the customer.

reply_language_consistent.description: True only after every natural-language fragment of reply has been audited and uses response_language, excluding proper nouns, URLs and code.

intent.description: The customer's current underlying intent, inferred from the whole conversation rather than keywords.

conversation_phase.description: A concise semantic description of where the conversation currently stands; do not force it into a fixed funnel.

customer_readiness.description: A concise evidence-based assessment of the customer's present readiness to progress or commit.

customer_goal.description: Cumulative customer outcome in semantic terms; empty only when it is genuinely unknown.

delivery_surfaces.description: Where the proposed solution itself operates.

conversation_channels.description: Where end users converse with the assistant.

handoff_destinations.description: Where human operators receive escalated conversations.

required_capabilities.description: Cumulative capabilities confirmed or requested by the customer.

operational_constraints.description: Cumulative constraints, dependencies, assumptions, and material unknowns.

material_blocker.description: The single genuinely material unresolved issue preventing the next commitment, or an empty string when none exists.

next_best_action.description: The best concrete customer-visible action to perform in this reply. It must create an externally observable outcome, not describe internal note-taking, preparation, planning, or future work. When the customer is ready to proceed and no material blocker remains, it must include one grounded recommendation or value synthesis and one explicit, easy-to-answer commitment request.

commercial_progress.description: The concrete customer decision, commitment, objection resolution, or support outcome this reply should create. Internal assistant activity, acknowledgment, paraphrase, or a promise to prepare something later is not progress.

decision_status.description: Semantic state of the customer's decision. Explicit acceptance of the immediately preceding proposal counts as agreement; a compatible refinement does not revoke it.

confirmed_commitments.description: Cumulative customer-approved decisions and constraints from the conversation. Preserve compatible prior agreements and never reopen unchanged commitments.

pending_customer_commitment.description: At most one genuinely unresolved customer action required to progress. Use null when none remains. An optional implementation detail, repeated final confirmation, or internal planning step is not a pending customer commitment.

should_escalate.description: Set true only when the runtime must persist and dispatch a human handoff. A direct request to stop automation and speak with a human is sufficient. Never claim a handoff in reply while this is false.

escalation_reason.description: Concise operational reason when should_escalate is true; null otherwise.

action_id.description: LLM-selected commercial action. request_required_fact is valid only for a fact explicitly identified as mandatory by authoritative supplied context.

selection_reason.description: Semantic reason this is the nearest useful commercial step, grounded in explicit customer commitments and supplied context.

requested_commitment.description: One concrete customer commitment requested by the selected action; never a repeated or invented prerequisite.

grounding_reference.description: A short literal excerpt from supplied authoritative context that proves either the exact total or that the missing fact is mandatory.

missing_fact.description: Only for request_required_fact: the single genuinely mandatory fact still absent. Null for all other actions.

authorization_is_explicit.description: Whether the newest inbound message explicitly authorizes this exact commercial action.

authorization_is_unambiguous.description: Whether no other service, scope, price, payment option, or next step could reasonably be meant.

authorization_evidence.description: Short evidence from the newest inbound message; empty when authorization is absent.

authorization_scope.description: The exact service, option, amount, and action authorized; empty when unresolved.

commercial_next_step.description: LLM-selected commercial progression grounded in conversation, memory, RAG, private policy, and verified tool results.

customer_outcome.description: The business outcome the customer is trying to achieve, stated without inventing facts.

recommended_step.description: The single best reversible next step selected semantically by the model.

why_now.description: Why this step is appropriate now, based on current evidence.

customer_commitment.description: The concrete commitment requested from the customer, if any.

seller_commitment.description: The concrete commitment offered by the company, if any.

instructions.description: Grounded operational payment instructions, never a canned response.

active_workstream.description: Concise semantic description of the outcome or service currently being discussed.

workstream_changed.description: True only when the customer materially changed the active outcome or service in this turn.
